originalTitle: Guardian journalists win across categories at Press Awards in London
summary: Political editor Pippa Crerar and features writer Simon Hattenstone top major categories and Malak A Tantesh wins for Gaza reporting
content: The Guardian’s political editor, a prominent features writer and a brave young Palestinian reporter are among those to have been honoured at the Press Awards in London. The awards celebrate the best journalism across all news media publishers distributing in the UK. Pippa Crerar, the Guardian’s political editor, won Political Journalist of the Year, for work including an exclusive in 2025 on Downing Street rethinking winter fuel payment cuts after Labour’s losses in local elections. Simon Hattenstone, a prominent features writer for the Guardian, scooped Broadsheet Feature Writer of the Year. His moving account of the demise of gifted musician Joe Black, who was badly let down by his homeless hostel, was highlighted at the awards. Malak A Tantesh’s harrowing report of her two years in besieged Gaza, where she shared her experience of living with perpetual fear and uncertainty in what she described as a “war that became a gateway to hell”, won her the Young Journalist of the Year. Tantesh regularly reported from Gaza for the Guardian in the aftermath of the 7 October attack, providing a vital and rare insight at a time when international journalists were mostly banned from Gaza. Other Guardian winners included data projects editor Pamela Duncan, who won Data Journalist of the Year for her investigation into how everyday Facebook networks fuel right-wing ideas, and Saturday magazine’s Charlotte Edwardes, who went home with the Broadsheet Interviewer of the Year award. The Guardian’s podcast team won News Podcast of the Year for Missing in the Amazon, which investigated the disappearance and murder of British journalist Dom Phillips and Brazilian indigenous expert Bruno Pereira in the remote Javari Valley in 2022. Phillips reported for the Guardian from Brazil. The Guardian also won Broadsheet Front Page of the Year for American Dread. Daily Newspaper of the Year was won by The Times.
chineseTitle: 《卫报》记者在伦敦新闻奖中荣获跨类别奖项
chineseSummary: 政治编辑 Pippa Crerar 和专题作家 Simon Hattenstone 名列主要类别榜首，Malak A Tantesh 因加沙报道而获奖
chineseContent: 《卫报》的政治编辑、一位著名的专题作家和一位勇敢的年轻巴勒斯坦记者都在伦敦新闻奖上获得殊荣。该奖项旨在表彰在英国发行的所有新闻媒体出版商中的最佳新闻作品。 《卫报》的政治编辑皮帕·克雷拉 (Pippa Crerar) 荣获年度政治记者奖，其工作成果包括 2025 年在唐宁街独家报道工党在地方选举中失利后重新考虑冬季燃油支付削减的问题。 《卫报》的著名专题作家西蒙·哈滕斯通 (Simon Hattenstone) 荣获年度大报专题作家奖。他对天才音乐家乔·布莱克（Joe Black）去世的感人描述，乔·布莱克因无家可归的旅馆而深感失望，这一点在颁奖典礼上得到了强调。马拉克·阿·坦特什 (Malak A Tantesh) 讲述了她在被围困的加沙两年的悲惨经历，分享了她在“通往地狱的战争”中生活在永久恐惧和不确定之中的经历，这为她赢得了年度青年记者奖。 10 月 7 日袭击事件发生后，坦特什定期在加沙为《卫报》进行报道，在国际记者大多被禁止进入加沙的情况下，他提供了重要且罕见的见解。其他《卫报》获奖者包括数据项目编辑帕梅拉·邓肯（Pamela Duncan）和《周六》杂志的夏洛特·爱德华兹（Charlotte Edwardes），前者因调查日常 Facebook 网络如何助长右翼思想而获得年度数据记者奖，后者获得年度大报采访者奖。 《卫报》的播客团队因《亚马逊失踪》而荣获年度新闻播客奖，该播客调查了 2022 年英国记者多姆·菲利普斯 (Dom Phillips) 和巴西土著专家布鲁诺·佩雷拉 (Bruno Pereira) 在偏远的贾瓦里山谷失踪和被谋杀的事件。菲利普斯在巴西为《卫报》进行报道。卫报
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
